name: KAG
tagline: Knowledge-graph reasoning and Q&A framework for professional domains
metaDescription: KAG is an open-source knowledge-graph reasoning and Q&A framework built on the OpenSPG engine and LLMs, made for multi-hop fact answers in expert domains.
overview: KAG (Knowledge Augmented Generation) is a logical reasoning and question-answering framework built on the OpenSPG engine and large language models. It is designed to build reasoning and Q&A solutions over vertical-domain knowledge bases, where unstructured documents, structured records, and expert rules all need to work together.

Plain vector RAG often struggles with ambiguity, and GraphRAG built from open information extraction can add noise. KAG aims to reduce both problems by linking knowledge to text chunks through a mutual index and by using logical forms to guide how a question is planned, retrieved, and reasoned over. It supports multi-hop fact questions and reports results that beat earlier methods on its benchmarks.
features: Knowledge and chunk mutual indexing that ties graph structure back to the original text for richer context
Schema-constrained knowledge construction so domain expert rules and entity/event types can be represented
Logical-form-guided hybrid solver with planning, reasoning, and retrieval operators
Combines four problem-solving paths: exact-match retrieval, text retrieval, numerical calculation, and semantic reasoning
Supports both private knowledge bases and public web sources through the MCP protocol
Knowledge alignment via conceptual semantic reasoning to cut down on extraction noise

gettingStarted.intro: KAG offers a product mode for ordinary users (run the full engine with Docker Compose) and a toolkit mode for developers (install the Python package). The toolkit path below is the quickest way to start building on macOS or Linux.

steps.heading: Launch the engine with Docker Compose
steps.body: Download the docker-compose file and start the OpenSPG engine and its dependent services. Then open the product UI at http://127.0.0.1:8887 (default login openspg / openspg@kag).
steps.code:
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenSPG/openspg/refs/heads/master/dev/release/docker-compose-west.yml -o docker-compose-west.yml
docker compose -f docker-compose-west.yml up -d

steps.heading: Create a Python environment
steps.body: For the developer toolkit, create and activate a Python 3.10 environment using conda.
steps.code: conda create -n kag-demo python=3.10 && conda activate kag-demo

steps.heading: Clone and install KAG
steps.body: Clone the repository and install the package in editable mode so you can use the built-in components.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/OpenSPG/KAG.git
cd KAG && pip install -e .

useCases: Building factual Q&A over a private, vertical-domain knowledge base that mixes documents and structured data
Answering multi-hop questions that need several reasoning steps rather than a single similarity lookup
Injecting domain expert rules and a custom schema to constrain how knowledge is extracted and reasoned over
Powering inference-driven Q&A inside agent workflows by exposing KAG over the MCP protocol
